name: Data Transparency
definition: Statements describing the lifecycle and flow of personal information, including its collection sources, categories, processing purposes, external sharing or disclosure, and retention standards.

children.name: Scope of PI Collection
children.definition: Statements describing the specific categories of personal information a business collects, including declarations that certain types of data are not collected.
children.example: We collect contact information such as your name and email address, as well as professional details like your job title.

children.name: Sources of PI Collected
children.definition: Statements identifying the entities, channels, or methods through which the business obtains consumers' personal information.
children.example: We receive information about you from social media platforms when you use them to log in to our portal.

children.name: Scope of PI Sold/Shared/Disclosed
children.definition: Statements specifying the categories of personal information that a business sells, shares for advertising, or discloses to third parties.
children.example: In the past 12 months, we have disclosed identifiers and commercial information to our service providers.

children.name: Third-Party Recipients of PI
children.definition: Statements identifying the categories or specific identities of third parties that receive personal information through sharing or disclosure.
children.example: Your data may be shared with our advertising partners, cloud storage providers, and analytics firms.

children.name: Purposes for PI Processing
children.definition: Statements describing the business or commercial reasons why personal information is collected, used, or otherwise handled.
children.example: We process your usage data to maintain the security of our website and to improve our product features.

children.name: PI Retention Periods/Criteria
children.definition: Statements describing the duration for which personal information is stored or the criteria used to determine when it should be deleted.
children.example: We retain your account data for as long as your account is active or as needed to comply with legal obligations.

name: Rights Notice
definition: Statements informing consumers of their specific privacy rights and the scope of control they have over their personal information.

children.name: Right to Delete
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to request deletion of personal information.
children.example: You can request that we delete your personal information.

children.name: Right to Correct
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to request correction of inaccurate personal information.
children.example: You can request correction of inaccurate personal information.

children.name: Right to Know / Access
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to know what personal information is collected, used, disclosed, shared, or sold, and to access that personal information.
children.example: You have the right to know what personal information we collect and to access it.

children.name: Right to Non-Discrimination
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right not to receive discriminatory treatment or retaliation for exercising privacy rights.
children.example: We will not discriminate against you for exercising your privacy rights.

children.name: Right to Opt Out
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to opt out of the sale, sharing, disclosure, or other processing of personal information where required by applicable law.
children.example: You can opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information.

children.name: Right to Limit SPI Use
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to limit the use or disclosure of sensitive personal information.
children.example: You can limit our use of your sensitive personal information.

children.name: Right to Data Portability
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to obtain personal information in a portable and readily usable format and, where technically feasible, to transmit it to another controller.
children.example: You can request a copy of your data in a portable format.

children.name: Right to Contest Profiling
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to question, review, and seek correction of profiling decisions that produce legal or similarly significant effects.
children.example: You can contest decisions based on profiling that significantly affect you.

children.name: Right to Obtain a List of Specific Third Parties
children.definition: Statements describing a consumer's right to obtain a list of specific third parties to which the controller has disclosed the consumer's personal data, or a list of specific third parties receiving consumers' personal data where consumer-specific records are not maintained.
children.example: You can request a list of specific third parties to whom we disclosed your personal data.

name: Privacy Controls
definition: Statements describing the administrative channels for consumer communication and the specialized operational safeguards for high-risk data processing, such as biometric information.

children.name: Contact Information
children.definition: Statements providing specific channels (email, phone, address, or forms) through which consumers can communicate with the business regarding privacy matters.
children.example: Contact us at privacy@example.com.

children.name: Appeal Mechanism
children.definition: Statements describing the formal process for consumers to challenge a business's decision regarding their privacy requests.
children.example: If we deny your request, you may appeal by emailing our legal department.

children.name: Biometric Data Security Incident Response Protocol
children.definition: Statements describing specific technical or organizational measures and notification procedures for addressing security breaches involving biometric data.
children.example: In the event of a biometric data breach, we follow a specialized incident response plan to notify affected users.

children.name: Biometric Deletion Guidelines
children.definition: Statements detailing the specific requirements, timelines, or technical procedures for the permanent removal of biometric identifiers.
children.example: We implement permanent deletion of biometric templates once the authentication purpose is fulfilled.

name: Policy Administration
definition: Statements identifying the legal entity responsible for data processing and those concerning the effective status, version control, and update procedures of the privacy policy itself.

children.name: Last Updated Date/Effective Date
children.definition: Statements that specify an explicit last updated date or effective date for the privacy policy.
children.example: Effective Date: January 1, 2026.

children.name: Controller Identification
children.definition: Statements that identify the legal entity responsible for deciding the purposes and means of processing personal information, including explicit naming or equivalent responsibility statements.
children.example: ABC Corp. is responsible for processing your personal information.

children.name: Notification Process of Material Change
children.definition: Statements describing the methods and channels through which consumers will be notified of significant or material updates to the privacy policy.
children.example: We will notify you of material changes by posting an updated notice on our website or sending an email.
